reasoning:
Verdict: REJECT (10% confidence)

Module Scores:
  Sentinel (CWE/OWASP Web Application Security): 50/100 [Low risk] — 1 info
  Watchdog (OWASP LLM Top 10 / Cisco AI Threat Taxonomy): 99/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Guardian (NIST AI RMF / EU AI Act / UNICC Responsible AI): 24/100 [Critical risk] — 3 critical, 6 high, 1 medium

Arbitration Triggers:
  • REJECT trigger — score below 30: Guardian (24/100)
  • REJECT trigger — critical findings in Guardian: [guardian-1] Repository contains no governance-relevant artifacts (no README, policies, or system documentation); [guardian-5] No privacy notice, data retention policy, or consent management for user-uploaded content and monitored social media data; [guardian-7] Minimal human oversight for automated content moderation and authenticity judgments

Disagreement Resolution:
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Sentinel scored 50/100 (low risk) — Δ49 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Sentinel to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Risk-level conflict: Guardian rates critical risk while Sentinel rates low risk on the same application. Council defers to Guardian's stricter assessment.
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 24/100 (critical risk) — Δ75 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.

Confidence Factors:
  • Base: 1/3 module(s) independently support REJECT — 30% baseline conviction
  • -30% confidence: 3 disagreement(s) between modules
  • Average module score across 3 completed module(s): 58/100 (reported for context — not directly used in confidence calibration)

--- LLM-Enhanced Council Narrative ---
Across modules, the strongest point of agreement is that the assessment is evidence-blocked: Sentinel (sentinel-1-dc981cc2) and Watchdog (watchdog-1) cannot validate security/LLM risks without code, configs, or dependency manifests, and Guardian (guardian-1) similarly cannot substantiate governance, privacy, or oversight claims because the repository contains no artifacts. This converges on a common operational reality: the true implementation and risk controls are unknown, so any assurance level is low and the residual risk should be treated as unbounded until artifacts are provided.

There is a severity conflict driven by different framing. Sentinel labels overall risk “low” largely because findings are informational and no concrete CWE/OWASP issues can be confirmed—while Guardian rates risk “critical” based on the described high-impact use case (media authenticity + moderation affecting public discourse) combined with missing governance fundamentals (guardian-5, guardian-7, guardian-8). Watchdog’s “info” risk score similarly reflects inability to evidence LLM-specific issues, but does not counter Guardian’s non-LLM AI governance concerns (ResNet/BERT provenance, fairness, transparency). Key gaps not fully covered by any module include software supply-chain and AWS/IaC security posture (SBOM, CI/CD, IAM/KMS, container hardening), adversarial robustness and model abuse risks for the stated non-LLM ML models (evasion, extraction, poisoning), and explicit rights/ToS compliance for social-media ingestion and user-uploaded content handling.

top_findings: Insufficient source code to assess VeriMedia AI (Flask/Python on AWS)
sentinel.summary: VeriMedia AI is described as a Flask/Python, AWS-deployed, public-facing REST API with PostgreSQL and user-uploaded media processing. However, no source code or configuration artifacts were provided, so security posture cannot be evaluated against CWE/OWASP requirements using static analysis.
sentinel.recommendation: 1) Provide the full codebase and dependency manifests so authentication/session handling, input validation, file upload security, and API controls can be verified (Finding: Insufficient source code to assess VeriMedia AI). 2) Provide deployment/IaC and runtime configuration (Docker/Terraform/ALB/API Gateway) to assess TLS, security headers, network exposure, and secret management (Finding: Insufficient source code to assess VeriMedia AI). 3) Provide database schema/migrations and data-access cod

top_findings: No-source-code condition prevents verification of any LLM integrations or OWASP LLM Top 10 exposures
watchdog.summary: VeriMedia AI: No source code was available, and the provided application profile claims no explicit LLM integrations. Therefore, Watchdog cannot identify or evidence any LLM-specific vulnerabilities, cannot name the exact LLM technology in use (if any), and cannot reference file paths/line numbers as required.
watchdog.recommendation: 1) Provide source files/dependency manifests to confirm whether any LLM libraries or hosted LLM endpoints are used (ties to finding: unknown LLM surface). 2) If claim extraction/fact-checking/moderation uses an LLM, share the exact prompt/tooling/retrieval implementation so OWASP-LLM01/02/06/07/08 can be assessed with concrete file/line evidence. 3) If there is truly no LLM usage, confirm by sharing requirements/lockfiles and inference client code so LLM-related attack surface can be formally ru

top_findings: Repository contains no governance-relevant artifacts (no README, policies, or system documentation)
No EU AI Act risk classification or Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment for media authenticity and moderation use case
No model cards or provenance documentation for ResNet/BERT deep learning models used for deepfake detection and fact verification
guardian.summary: VeriMedia AI is described as a Flask/Python media authenticity and moderation platform using ResNet and BERT, with social media monitoring and autonomous decisions affecting public discourse. The repository contains no files at all, resulting in a critical governance failure: there is no way to evidence documentation, privacy controls, bias testing, human oversight, auditability, or regulatory alignment. Based on the stated functionality, the system presents high societal and fundamental-rights
guardian.recommendation: 1) Establish a complete governance documentation baseline (README.md, risk assessment, EU AI Act assessment/FRIA, privacy/DPIA) to satisfy NIST GOVERN/MAP and EUAI-RISK-CLASS obligations (Findings: documentation absence; EU AI Act assessment; privacy). 2) Implement and document human oversight, appeals, and audit logging/incident response for moderation and authenticity decisions (Findings: minimal human oversight; lack of accountability) aligned to EUAI-OVERSIGHT and NIST-MANAGE. 3) Create mode
